title: Cromidon minutum
intro: Cromidon minutum is a species of succulent plant native to South Africa, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. Traditionally, Cromidon minutum has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cromidon minutum include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Cromidon minutum is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are consumed to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.

Cromidon minutum is native to the Western Cape province of South Africa, where it grows in dry, rocky areas.
